red_lines: Axis domination of Europe or Asia-Pacific
Unconditional surrender not achieved from Axis powers
core_narratives: Arsenal of democracy defending the Four Freedoms
United Nations as framework for lasting peace
America's industrial might as decisive war factor
Self-determination of peoples against fascist tyranny

decision_history.event_description: Japan attacked Pearl Harbor destroying much of the Pacific Fleet
decision_history.decision_taken: Requested and obtained congressional declaration of war; adopted Europe First strategy despite Pacific attack
decision_history.outcome: Full American mobilization; Europe First ensured Germany's defeat took priority
context_factors: assessment that Germany was greater long-term threat

decision_history.event_description: Need to decide between immediate cross-Channel invasion or peripheral strategy
decision_history.decision_taken: Approved Operation Torch - invasion of French North Africa as compromise with British strategy
decision_history.outcome: Axis expelled from North Africa by May 1943; delayed cross-Channel attack to 1944
context_factors: British insistence troops not ready for France
need for American troops to see combat in 1942
Vichy French North Africa as softer target

decision_history.event_description: Casablanca Conference with Churchill to set Allied war strategy
decision_history.decision_taken: Announced unconditional surrender policy for all Axis powers
decision_history.outcome: Eliminated possibility of negotiated peace; unified Allied war aims but may have stiffened Axis resistance
context_factors: desire to reassure Stalin of Western commitment

cultural_context.domestic_power_base: New Deal coalition (labor unions, urban voters, farmers, Southern Democrats), Democratic Party majority in Congress

military_posture.force_disposition: massive industrial mobilization; force projection building across both oceans
